media.name: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold buffered, with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source (PMC11879879)
media.base_medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM), Hartmans / de Bont ('HdB') mineral medium, here used 4-fold phosphate-buffered with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source; host Pseudomonas putida KT2440
media.base_medium_reference: Paper (Nat. Microbiol. 2025, doi:10.1038/s41564-025-01929-5, PMID 39929973) cites its MSM to ref 57 = Wierckx et al. 'Engineering of solvent-tolerant Pseudomonas putida S12 for bioproduction of phenol from glucose', Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 71:8221-8227 (2005), doi:10.1128/AEM.71.12.8221-8227.2005, PMID 16332806, PMC1317433. Wierckx 2005 in turn defines MSM ('37 mM phosphate buffer, pH 7.0') per Hartmans et al., Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 55:2850-2855 (1989), doi:10.1128/aem.55.11.2850-2855.1989, PMID 16348047, PMC203180. The full de Bont-group MSM base composition (N source, Mg, trace-element solution) corresponds to the Hartmans-de Bont (HdB) mineral medium, Hartmans, Kaptein, Tramper & de Bont, Appl. Microbiol. Biotechnol. 37:796-801 (1992), doi:10.1007/BF00174849.
media.verification_evidence: P. putida KT2440 strains were cultivated in 4-fold buffered (15.52 g l-1 K2HPO4 and 6.52 g l-1 NaH2PO4) MSM medium ... MSM containing 15 mM of HMDA, Ahx or e-caprolactam as sole carbon source.
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC11879879 (see GrowthDB record).
